sections.heading: Symptoms
sections.content: Pancreatic adenocarcinoma often presents late with non-specific symptoms, leading to delayed diagnosis. The classic presentation includes painless jaundice for tumors in the pancreatic head, weight loss, and new-onset diabetes. Symptoms often develop insidiously and are nonspecific, contributing to late-stage diagnosis [MS-5].

symptoms.name: Jaundice
symptoms.detail: Painless obstructive jaundice is a hallmark for pancreatic head tumors, caused by bile duct obstruction. It is often the presenting symptom leading to diagnosis.

symptoms.name: Abdominal pain
symptoms.detail: Dull, epigastric pain that may radiate to the back, often worse after meals and when lying down. Caused by tumor invasion of retroperitoneal nerves or obstruction of the pancreatic duct.

symptoms.name: Unintentional weight loss
symptoms.detail: Significant and unintentional weight loss is a frequent symptom, often due to malabsorption, anorexia, and catabolic state.

symptoms.name: Anorexia
symptoms.detail: Loss of appetite is common and may be related to tumor effects, pain, or treatment.

symptoms.name: Nausea and vomiting
symptoms.detail: May be caused by gastric outlet obstruction from a pancreatic head tumor, or as a side effect of treatments.
symptoms.frequency: Less common, can be a late symptom

symptoms.name: Pancreatitis
symptoms.detail: New-onset pancreatitis can be the first manifestation of a pancreatic tumor obstructing the pancreatic duct.

symptoms.name: Pruritus
symptoms.detail: Generalized itching due to bile salt deposition in the skin from obstructive jaundice.

symptoms.name: Jaundice (sign)
symptoms.detail: Yellow discoloration of skin and sclera.

symptoms.name: Palpable gallbladder (Courvoisier sign)
symptoms.detail: A non-tender, distended gallbladder palpable in the right upper quadrant, indicative of distal bile duct obstruction.
symptoms.frequency: Variable, more common in pancreatic head tumors

symptoms.name: Ascites
symptoms.detail: Fluid accumulation in the abdomen, indicating peritoneal dissemination or portal hypertension.
symptoms.frequency: Uncommon at presentation, more common in advanced disease

symptoms.name: Cachexia
symptoms.detail: Severe weight loss and muscle wasting.

alarms.name: Jaundice
alarms.detail: Painless obstructive jaundice is a hallmark for pancreatic head tumors, caused by bile duct obstruction. It is often the presenting symptom leading to diagnosis.

alarms.name: Abdominal pain
alarms.detail: Dull, epigastric pain that may radiate to the back, often worse after meals and when lying down. Caused by tumor invasion of retroperitoneal nerves or obstruction of the pancreatic duct.

alarms.name: Unintentional weight loss
alarms.detail: Significant and unintentional weight loss is a frequent symptom, often due to malabsorption, anorexia, and catabolic state.

alarms.name: Nausea and vomiting
alarms.detail: May be caused by gastric outlet obstruction from a pancreatic head tumor, or as a side effect of treatments.

alarms.name: Pancreatitis
alarms.detail: New-onset pancreatitis can be the first manifestation of a pancreatic tumor obstructing the pancreatic duct.

alarms.name: Pruritus
alarms.detail: Generalized itching due to bile salt deposition in the skin from obstructive jaundice.

alarms.name: Red Flag
alarms.detail: New-onset diabetes in an older adult without classic risk factors

alarms.name: Red Flag
alarms.detail: Deep vein thrombrosis (Trousseau syndrome) as a paraneoplastic phenomenon
